title: Matching Quality Dashboard
goal: Check whether matching/experiment assignment data is usable: group sizes, duplicate users, date coverage, and joins to events/orders.
business_context: Before comparing product groups, the team must trust that users are assigned correctly and that joins do not create obvious data problems.

related_portfolio_templates.title: Matching quality dashboard writeup
related_portfolio_templates.what_to_write: Describe assignment quality, group sizes, duplicate users, join coverage, and which checks are needed before any A/B conclusion.
related_portfolio_templates.chart_or_table: Include a group-size bar chart, an average order by group chart/table, and a small data-quality checklist.
related_portfolio_templates.readme_bullet: Built a matching-quality dashboard in pandas/matplotlib, validating experiment assignment data before deeper product comparison.

milestones.title: Inspect matching data
milestones.description: Load df_matching.csv and inspect shape, columns, missing values, group sizes, and date range.
dataset_hints: df_matching.csv contains user_id, exp_group, and event_date.
milestones.starter_code:
import pandas as pd

matching = pd.read_csv("datasets/df_matching.csv")
print(matching.shape)
print(matching.columns.tolist())
print(matching.isna().sum())
print(matching["exp_group"].value_counts())
print(matching["event_date"].min(), matching["event_date"].max())
checklist: I wrote one quality concern or confirmed none found.

milestones.title: Check user assignment uniqueness
milestones.description: Verify whether each user appears once and whether any user has multiple experiment groups.
dataset_hints: Use groupby user_id and nunique exp_group.
milestones.starter_code:
user_rows = matching.groupby("user_id").size()
user_groups = matching.groupby("user_id")["exp_group"].nunique()
print("duplicate user rows:", int((user_rows > 1).sum()))
print("users in multiple groups:", int((user_groups > 1).sum()))
checklist: I checked users in multiple groups.
I explained whether the assignment table is safe to join.

milestones.title: Join matching to orders
milestones.description: Join experiment groups to orders and calculate group-level order metrics. Focus on data sanity, not causal claims.
dataset_hints: Use user_id and left/inner join deliberately.
milestones.starter_code:
orders = pd.read_csv("datasets/df_orders.csv")
orders_with_group = orders.merge(matching[["user_id", "exp_group"]], on="user_id", how="left")
print(orders_with_group["exp_group"].isna().sum())
summary = orders_with_group.groupby("exp_group", as_index=False).agg(
    users=("user_id", "nunique"),
    orders=("order_id", "nunique"),
    revenue=("order_sum", "sum"),
    avg_order=("order_sum", "mean"),
)
print(summary)
checklist: I checked missing experiment groups after join.
I calculated users, orders, revenue, and average order.

milestones.title: Create quality dashboard visuals
milestones.description: Create two simple charts: group size bar chart and average order by group. Keep them clear and honest.
milestones.starter_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

group_sizes = matching["exp_group"].value_counts().sort_index()
ax = group_sizes.plot(kind="bar", figsize=(7, 4))
ax.set_title("Experiment group sizes")
ax.set_xlabel("Group")
ax.set_ylabel("Users")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

ax = summary.plot(x="exp_group", y="avg_order", kind="bar", legend=False, figsize=(7, 4))
ax.set_title("Average order by experiment group")
ax.set_xlabel("Group")
ax.set_ylabel("Average order")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
checklist: Charts have titles and axis labels.
The chart text does not claim impact without a test.

milestones.title: Write dashboard summary
milestones.description: Summarize whether matching data is safe for deeper group comparison and what should be checked before A/B conclusions.
dataset_hints: Mention uniqueness, group balance, join coverage, and metric limitations.
checklist: I stated whether matching quality is acceptable.
I listed risks before causal comparison.
I suggested the next experiment-analysis step.

portfolio_prompt: Write a dashboard-style case study showing whether experiment matching data is ready for analysis, including group balance, uniqueness checks, join coverage, and limitations.
